optionA.text: A pair of long grooved palps attached on the anterior dorsum

optionB.text: Two groups of grooved tentacular cirri present
At least some setae either curved hooks or spines

optionA.text: Acicular setae distally excavate; body usually dark green or brown

optionB.text: Acicular setae not excavate, body usually light colored

optionA.text: Acicular spines in posterior segments distally entire

optionB.text: Acicular spines in posterior segments distally bi- or multifid
At least some capillary setae present

optionA.text: Anterior region without long tentacular structures

optionB.text: One or more anterior segments with groups of long tentacular cirri or branchiae

optionA.text: Dorsal tentacular cirri first present posterior to the anteriormost branchiae

optionB.text: Dorsal tentacular cirri first present from the same segment as the anteriormost branchiae

optionA.text: Tentacular cirri on one segment only

optionB.text: Tentacular cirri on two or more segments
